title: Ayenia irwinii
taxonomy: Ayenia irwinii is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It belongs to the genus Ayenia, which is a group of plants native to the Americas. Within the Malpighiaceae family, Ayenia irwinii is classified as a member of the order Malpighiales. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaves and flowers, which are typical of the Malpighiales order. The Malpighiales order is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that are found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world.

intro: Ayenia irwinii, also known as the "Yerba de la Muerta" or "Death's Herb", is a shrub-like plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Ayenia irwinii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to be responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Ayenia irwinii is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, which can be found in herbal teas, tinctures, and capsules, although its use should be approached with caution due to limited scientific research.
